meta.originalTitle: Australian artist Margaret Preston 澳大利亚艺术家

children.text: You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1–13, which are based on Reading Passage 1 below.
Margaret Preston's vibrant paintings and prints of Australian flowers, animals and landscapes have delighted the Australian public since the early 1920s.

Margaret Preston was born Margaret Rose McPherson in Port Adelaide, South Australia in 1875, the daughter of David McPherson, a Scottish marine engineer and his wife Prudence Lyle. She and her sister were sent at first to a private school, but when family circumstances changed, her mother took the girls to Sydney where Margaret attended a public high school. She decided early in life to become an artist and took private art lessons. In 1888, she trained for several months with Sydney landscape painter William Lister, and in 1893 enrolled at the National Gallery of Victoria Art School, where she studied for just over four years.

In 1898, after her father died, Margaret returned to Adelaide to study and then teach at the Adelaide School of Design. Her early artwork was influenced by the German aesthetic tradition, in which subjects of the natural world were depicted in a true-to-life manner. Margaret's first visit to Europe in 1904, and her studies in Paris, France had little impact on this naturalism that dominated her work from this early period. However, some eight years later, after returning to Paris, she began to recognise the decorative possibilities of art.

With the outbreak of the First World War, Margaret travelled to England, where she had exhibitions and continued her studies of art. She was a student of pottery, but at some time developed her interest in various techniques of printmaking and design. In England's West Country, she taught basket weaving at a rehabilitation unit for servicemen. It was on board a boat returning to Australia that she met wealthy businessman William Preston, whom she married in 1919. Together Margaret and William settled in the Sydney harbourside suburb of Mosman. The most characteristic prints from her early years in Sydney are views of boats floating on Sydney Harbour and of houses clustered on foreshore hills. Although Sydney was their home, the couple travelled regularly, both overseas and within Australia.

Her first major showing in Australia was with her friend Thea Proctor, in exhibitions in Melbourne and Sydney in 1925. Many of Preston's prints were hand-coloured in rich scarlet reds, blues and greens, and all of them were set in Chinese red lacquer frames. Harbour views were again prominent, but in comparison with earlier artworks, they were compact and busy, using striking contrasts of black and white combined with elaborate patterns and repetitions. Other prints from this period featured native flora. It was with these still-life subjects that she convinced the public that Australian native flowers were equal in beauty to any exotic species.

From 1932 to 1939, Preston moved away from Sydney and lived with her husband at Berowra, on the upper reaches of the Hawkesbury River. The area was one of rugged natural beauty, and for the first time Preston found herself living in a home surrounded by bush. Prior to this, the native flowers that featured in her paintings and prints had been purchased from local florists; they now grew in abundance around her home. Preston's prints became larger, less complex and less reliant on the use of bright colours. Flowers were no longer arranged in vases, and Preston began to concentrate instead on flowers that were growing wild.

While living at Berowra, and undoubtedly prompted by the Aboriginal rock engravings found near her property, Preston also developed what was to be a lifelong interest in Aboriginal art. On returning to Sydney in 1939, she became a member of the Anthropological Society of New South Wales, and later visited many important Aboriginal sites throughout Australia. Preston believed that Aboriginal art provided the key to establishing a national body of art that reflected the vast and ancient continent of Australia.

During the 1940s, symbols used by Aboriginal people, together with dried, burnt colours found in traditional Aboriginal paintings, became increasingly prominent in her prints. The artist's titles from this period frequently acknowledge her sources, and reveal the extent to which she drew inspiration from traditional Aboriginal art to create her own art.

It was in 1953, at the age of 78, that Preston produced her most significant prints. The exhibition at Macquarie Galleries in Sydney included 29 prints made using the ancient technique known as stencilling. Many of the artworks in the exhibition incorporated her fusion of Aboriginal and Chinese concepts. Preston had admired Chinese art since 1915, when she acquired the first of her many books on the subject, and she had visited China on two occasions. Chinese elements may be found in several of her earlier paintings.

However, in her prints of the 1950s, Preston combined Chinese ideas with her understanding of the Dreamtime creation stories of Aboriginal Australians. Preston did not let age alter her habit of working hard. As she got older, her love of painting, printmaking and travel continued. By the time of her death in 1963, when she was 88, she had produced over 400 paintings and prints. In a career spanning almost 60 years, she created a body of work that demonstrates her extraordinary originality and the intensity of her commitment to Australian art.
Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 1–7 on your answer sheet, write:
if the statement agrees with the information
if the statement contradicts the information
if there is no information on this
Artists in the German aesthetic tradition portrayed nature realistically.
Margaret attended a famous art college in Paris.
Margaret met her husband William while teaching a craft at a rehabilitation unit.
Margaret Preston and Thea Proctor explored similar themes in their art.
Margaret's 1925 artworks of Sydney Harbour were simpler than her previous ones.
The colours in Margaret's Berowra prints were very bright.
When living in Berowra, Margaret painted flowers in their natural location.

explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：遵循德意志审美传统的艺术家以写实手法描绘自然。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “Her early artwork was influenced by the German aesthetic tradition, in which subjects of the natural world were depicted in a true-to-life manner”，“true-to-life” 意为 “写实的”，明确说明德意志审美传统的艺术家以写实手法描绘自然事物，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
题目翻译：玛格丽特曾就读于巴黎一所著名的艺术院校。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：Paragraph 3 和 Paragraph 8 均提到玛格丽特 “在巴黎学习艺术”，但未提及她就读的是 “著名艺术院校”，既无学校名称，也无 “famous” 相关评价，无法确认院校知名度，信息缺失，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
题目翻译：玛格丽特在康复中心教授手工艺时，结识了丈夫威廉。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “In England’s West Country, she taught basket weaving at a rehabilitation unit for servicemen. It was on board a boat returning to Australia that she met wealthy businessman William Preston”，可知她在康复中心教授编篮，但在 “返回澳大利亚的船上” 结识威廉，而非在康复中心相遇，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：玛格丽特・普雷斯顿与西娅・普罗克特在艺术创作中探讨相似主题。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “Her first major showing in Australia was with her friend Thea Proctor, in exhibitions in Melbourne and Sydney in 1925”，仅提到两人 1925 年联合办展，但未对比他们的艺术 “主题是否相似”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
题目翻译：玛格丽特 1925 年创作的悉尼港主题作品，比她早期的作品更简洁。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “in comparison with earlier artworks, they were compact and busy, using striking contrasts of black and white combined with elaborate patterns and repetitions”，“compact and busy”“elaborate patterns” 意为 “紧凑、内容丰富、图案精致”，说明 1925 年的港口主题作品比早期更复杂，而非 “更简洁”，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：玛格丽特在贝罗拉时期创作的版画，色彩非常鲜艳。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “Preston's prints became larger, less complex and less reliant on the use of bright colours”，明确指出贝罗拉时期的版画 “不再依赖鲜艳色彩”，即色彩不明亮，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：居住在贝罗拉期间，玛格丽特描绘生长在自然环境中的花卉。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “Flowers were no longer arranged in vases, and Preston began to concentrate instead on flowers that were growing wild”，“growing wild” 意为 “野生生长”，即她直接描绘自然环境中生长的花卉，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

items.text:
（1）题目 1：Artists in the German aesthetic tradition portrayed nature realistically.
题目翻译：遵循德意志审美传统的艺术家以写实手法描绘自然。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 3 中 “Her early artwork was influenced by the German aesthetic tradition, in which subjects of the natural world were depicted in a true-to-life manner”，“true-to-life” 意为 “写实的”，明确说明德意志审美传统的艺术家以写实手法描绘自然事物，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

（2）题目 2：Margaret attended a famous art college in Paris.
题目翻译：玛格丽特曾就读于巴黎一所著名的艺术院校。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：Paragraph 3 和 Paragraph 8 均提到玛格丽特 “在巴黎学习艺术”，但未提及她就读的是 “著名艺术院校”，既无学校名称，也无 “famous” 相关评价，无法确认院校知名度，信息缺失，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

（3）题目 3：Margaret met her husband William while teaching a craft at a rehabilitation unit.
题目翻译：玛格丽特在康复中心教授手工艺时，结识了丈夫威廉。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 4 中 “In England’s West Country, she taught basket weaving at a rehabilitation unit for servicemen. It was on board a boat returning to Australia that she met wealthy businessman William Preston”，可知她在康复中心教授编篮，但在 “返回澳大利亚的船上” 结识威廉，而非在康复中心相遇，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（4）题目 4：Margaret Preston and Thea Proctor explored similar themes in their art.
题目翻译：玛格丽特・普雷斯顿与西娅・普罗克特在艺术创作中探讨相似主题。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “Her first major showing in Australia was with her friend Thea Proctor, in exhibitions in Melbourne and Sydney in 1925”，仅提到两人 1925 年联合办展，但未对比他们的艺术 “主题是否相似”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

（5）题目 5：Margaret’s 1925 artworks of Sydney Harbour were simpler than her previous ones.
题目翻译：玛格丽特 1925 年创作的悉尼港主题作品，比她早期的作品更简洁。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 5 中 “in comparison with earlier artworks, they were compact and busy, using striking contrasts of black and white combined with elaborate patterns and repetitions”，“compact and busy”“elaborate patterns” 意为 “紧凑、内容丰富、图案精致”，说明 1925 年的港口主题作品比早期更复杂，而非 “更简洁”，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（6）题目 6：The colours in Margaret’s Berowra prints were very bright.
题目翻译：玛格丽特在贝罗拉时期创作的版画，色彩非常鲜艳。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “Preston's prints became larger, less complex and less reliant on the use of bright colours”，明确指出贝罗拉时期的版画 “不再依赖鲜艳色彩”，即色彩不明亮，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（7）题目 7：When living in Berowra, Margaret painted flowers in their natural location.
题目翻译：居住在贝罗拉期间，玛格丽特描绘生长在自然环境中的花卉。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 Paragraph 6 中 “Flowers were no longer arranged in vases, and Preston began to concentrate instead on flowers that were growing wild”，“growing wild” 意为 “野生生长”，即她直接描绘自然环境中生长的花卉，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

children.text: from the passage for each answer.
interest in Aboriginal art was inspired by seeing rock engravings close to her Berowra home
and colours from Aboriginal art in her own work
often referred to Aboriginal sources in the
about Chinese art that she had started collecting in 1915
combination of Chinese and Aboriginal elements
worked for nearly six decades, making more than
dedicated to Australian art, and the originality of her work is seen in Preston's long career

explanationSection.sectionTitle: 2. 笔记填空题（Questions 8–13：ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER）
explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：土著艺术影响 —— 在自己的作品中融入了来自土著艺术的_\___\__和色彩
答案：symbols（符号）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “During the 1940s, symbols used by Aboriginal people, together with dried, burnt colours found in traditional Aboriginal paintings, became increasingly prominent in her prints”，明确提到她在作品中融入土著人使用的 “符号（symbols）” 和色彩，因此答案为 “symbols”。
题目翻译：土著艺术影响 —— 常在为作品赋予的_\___\__中提及土著艺术来源
答案：titles（标题）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “The artist’s titles from this period frequently acknowledge her sources, and reveal the extent to which she drew inspiration from traditional Aboriginal art”，可知她通过作品的 “标题（titles）” 提及土著艺术这一灵感来源，因此答案为 “titles”。
题目翻译：1953 年展览 —— 部分版画采用了一种名为_\___\__的古老技法
答案：stencilling（模板印刷）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “the exhibition at Macquarie Galleries in Sydney included 29 prints made using the ancient technique known as stencilling”，“ancient technique” 对应题干 “very old method”，因此答案为 “stencilling”。
题目翻译：1953 年展览 —— 灵感来自她自 1915 年开始收集的关于中国艺术的_\___\__
答案：books（书籍）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “Preston had admired Chinese art since 1915, when she acquired the first of her many books on the subject”，“acquired the first of her many books” 对应 “started collecting books”，可知她受 “关于中国艺术的书籍” 启发，因此答案为 “books”。
题目翻译：晚年 —— 仍对艺术和_\___\__感兴趣
答案：travel（旅行）
解析：定位 Paragraph 9 中 “As she got older, her love of painting, printmaking and travel continued”，明确提到晚年她仍热爱 “绘画、版画制作和旅行”，题干中已提及 “art”，因此补充 “travel”。
题目翻译：晚年 —— 职业生涯近 60 年，创作了超过_\___\__件艺术品
答案：400（400 件）
解析：定位 Paragraph 9 中 “By the time of her death in 1963, when she was 88, she had produced over 400 paintings and prints”，“over 400” 对应 “more than 400”，且 “paintings and prints” 均属于 “artworks”，因此答案为 “400”。

items.text:
（1）题目 8：Aboriginal influence - incorporated \___\___ and colours from Aboriginal art in her own work
题目翻译：土著艺术影响 —— 在自己的作品中融入了来自土著艺术的_\___\__和色彩
答案：symbols（符号）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “During the 1940s, symbols used by Aboriginal people, together with dried, burnt colours found in traditional Aboriginal paintings, became increasingly prominent in her prints”，明确提到她在作品中融入土著人使用的 “符号（symbols）” 和色彩，因此答案为 “symbols”。

（2）题目 9：Aboriginal influence - often referred to Aboriginal sources in the \___\___ she gave her artworks
题目翻译：土著艺术影响 —— 常在为作品赋予的_\___\__中提及土著艺术来源
答案：titles（标题）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “The artist’s titles from this period frequently acknowledge her sources, and reveal the extent to which she drew inspiration from traditional Aboriginal art”，可知她通过作品的 “标题（titles）” 提及土著艺术这一灵感来源，因此答案为 “titles”。

（3）题目 10：1953 exhibition - a very old method called \___\___ was used for some prints
题目翻译：1953 年展览 —— 部分版画采用了一种名为_\___\__的古老技法
答案：stencilling（模板印刷）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “the exhibition at Macquarie Galleries in Sydney included 29 prints made using the ancient technique known as stencilling”，“ancient technique” 对应题干 “very old method”，因此答案为 “stencilling”。

（4）题目 11：1953 exhibition - was inspired by \___\___ about Chinese art that she had started collecting in 1915
题目翻译：1953 年展览 —— 灵感来自她自 1915 年开始收集的关于中国艺术的_\___\__
答案：books（书籍）
解析：定位 Paragraph 8 中 “Preston had admired Chinese art since 1915, when she acquired the first of her many books on the subject”，“acquired the first of her many books” 对应 “started collecting books”，可知她受 “关于中国艺术的书籍” 启发，因此答案为 “books”。

（5）题目 12：Old age - still interested in art and \___\___
题目翻译：晚年 —— 仍对艺术和_\___\__感兴趣
答案：travel（旅行）
解析：定位 Paragraph 9 中 “As she got older, her love of painting, printmaking and travel continued”，明确提到晚年她仍热爱 “绘画、版画制作和旅行”，题干中已提及 “art”，因此补充 “travel”。

（6）题目 13：Old age - worked for nearly six decades, making more than \___\___ artworks
题目翻译：晚年 —— 职业生涯近 60 年，创作了超过_\___\__件艺术品
答案：400（400 件）
解析：定位 Paragraph 9 中 “By the time of her death in 1963, when she was 88, she had produced over 400 paintings and prints”，“over 400” 对应 “more than 400”，且 “paintings and prints” 均属于 “artworks”，因此答案为 “400”。
